implementation_notes: Added test_execute_turn_writes_recovered_data_to_disk to the TestUnrecoverableErrors class in tests/unit/test_autobuild_orchestrator.py. The test follows the same fixture and pattern as the existing test_execute_turn_uses_state_recovery_for_recoverable test. It: (1) mocks the Player to return a recoverable error (success=False, unrecoverable=False), (2) mocks _attempt_state_recovery to return a successful recovered result, (3) mocks the Coach (invoke_coach) to return an approval so the turn completes, then (4) asserts that mock_agent_invoker._write_direct_mode_results was called exactly once with task_id='TASK-AB-001', the recovered report, and success=True. All 135 existing tests continue to pass with no regressions.
requirements_addressed: New test verifying _write_direct_mode_results is called when state recovery succeeds
Verified call uses correct arguments: task_id, recovered report, success=True
All existing tests pass with no regressions (135 total)

completion_promises.criterion_text: When state recovery succeeds, _write_direct_mode_results is called on the agent invoker with the recovered report data

completion_promises.evidence: The assert_called_once_with call includes success=True as a keyword argument

completion_promises.criterion_text: _write_direct_mode_results is called with the task_id and the recovered report
completion_promises.evidence: The assert_called_once_with call verifies positional args: task_id='TASK-AB-001' and recovered_result.report
All 135 tests pass (134 pre-existing + 1 new). Run: python3 -m pytest tests/unit/test_autobuild_orchestrator.py -v --tb=short
